title: Feed Cost Estimator
description: Industrial-grade calculation tool for estimating total feed cost per animal per day, incorporating raw material costs, processing losses, and quality adjustments. Aligned with ISO 22000, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Daily feed intake per animal (kg)
inputs.businessContext: Average daily feed consumption per animal, based on species and growth stage.

inputs.label: Raw material cost per kg
inputs.businessContext: Cost of primary feed ingredients (corn, soybean meal, etc.) per kilogram.

inputs.label: Processing loss (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of raw material lost during milling, mixing, pelleting, or storage.

inputs.label: Moisture adjustment factor
inputs.businessContext: Correction factor for moisture content variation in raw materials (1.0 = standard).

inputs.label: Quality grade
inputs.businessContext: Grade of feed based on nutrient density and consistency (premium, standard, economy).

inputs.label: Include transport cost?
inputs.businessContext: If true, adds a fixed transport surcharge per kg of feed.

rules.message: Feed intake must be greater than zero.
Raw material cost must be positive.
Processing loss must be between 0% and 15%.
Moisture adjustment factor must be between 0.85 and 1.15.

thresholds.message: Daily feed cost exceeds $5.00 per animal – review ingredient sourcing.
Processing loss above 8% – investigate equipment or process inefficiency.
Moisture adjustment factor deviates more than 8% from standard – verify moisture content.

formulas.label: Effective raw material cost per kg
formulas.description: Raw material cost adjusted for moisture and processing loss.

formulas.label: Quality grade multiplier
formulas.description: Multiplier based on quality grade (premium=1.15, standard=1.0, economy=0.90).

formulas.label: Adjusted feed cost per kg
formulas.description: Total cost per kg of feed delivered to animal, including quality adjustment.

formulas.label: Total feed cost per animal per day
formulas.description: Primary result: daily feed cost for one animal.

formulas.label: Cost per kg of weight gain
formulas.description: Feed cost efficiency metric (assumes feed conversion ratio of 2.5).

primary.label: Total feed cost per animal per day

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive processing loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Processing loss above 5% indicates potential equipment wear or improper settings.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Moisture variation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Moisture adjustment factor outside 0.95-1.05 range suggests inconsistent raw material quality.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Quality grade mismatch
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Using premium grade when standard would suffice increases cost unnecessarily.

suggestedActions.action: Implement preventive maintenance on mills and pellet mills to reduce processing loss below 3%.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Savings of up to 2% on raw material cost.

suggestedActions.action: Negotiate with suppliers for consistent moisture content or install on-line moisture sensors.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduction in cost variability and improved feed consistency.

suggestedActions.action: Evaluate if standard grade feed meets animal performance targets before using premium.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Potential 10-15% reduction in feed cost per kg.

suggestedActions.action: Consolidate raw material purchases to achieve volume discounts.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduction of raw material cost by 3-5%.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data confidence adjusted cost
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total feed cost adjusted by a confidence factor (0.9) to account for input variability.
